title: Check Missing Tests ŌĆö check_missing_test
text: data Data frame, test data check. Must prepared prepare_test_data standardized column names test_var Character string, variable name filtering specific tests (default: NULL). NULL, checks tests. Typically use "TESTDE" (test name) "TESTCAT" (test category) test Character vector, one specific values test_var (default: NULL). E.g., "RBC Count" c("RBC Count", "WBC Count"). Ignored test_var NULL missing_de Logical, whether check individual TESTDE missing (default: TRUE). TRUE, checks three missing types; FALSE, checks first two types (TESTCAT empty, entire TESTCAT missing) pdno Character string specifying protocol deviation number check (default: "8.3.1")

title: Check Missing Tests ŌĆö check_missing_test
text: List following components: has_deviation Logical. TRUE missing tests messages Character vector. Deviation description messages details Data frame. Missing test details columns: PDNO: Protocol deviation number specified pdno parameter SUBJID: Subject ID VISIT: Visit name VISITNUM: Visit number visit_date: Actual visit date TBNAME: Test dataset name (e.g., LB, VS, EG) test_name: Test name missing_type: Missing type DESCRIPTION: Detailed description missing test

title: Check Missing Tests ŌĆö check_missing_test
text: function checks corresponding tests completed actual visit records. recommended first prepare data using prepare_test_data:
code: # Step 1: Read config file (required) testconfig <- read_testconfig_file("config/test_config.xlsx")  # Step 2: Prepare test data (uses testconfig automatically) prepared_data <- prepare_test_data(   data = list(LB = lb_data, SV = sv_data),   test_dataset = "LB",   test_date_var = "LBDAT",   test_result_var = "ORRES" )  # Step 3: Check all tests (including overall missing and individual missing) result <- check_missing_test(data = prepared_data)  # Or check specific tests (e.g., RBC count) result <- check_missing_test(   data = prepared_data,   test_var = "TESTDE",   test = "RBC Count" )  # Or check multiple tests at once result <- check_missing_test(   data = prepared_data,   test_var = "TESTDE",   test = c("RBC Count", "WBC Count") )  # Only check overall test missing, not individual indicators result <- check_missing_test(   data = prepared_data,   missing_de = FALSE )

title: Check Missing Tests ŌĆö check_missing_test
text: function distinguishes three types missing: 1. TESTCAT empty (visit test records) Condition: TESTCAT empty NA Output: One record per subject/visit/TBNAME combination Format: e.g., "LB missing" Reason: "TBNAME test performed" 2. TESTCAT empty, entire TESTCAT missing (e.g., entire CBC done) Condition: TESTCAT empty, TESTDAT empty TESTYN != "Yes" Output: One record per subject/visit/TESTCAT combination Format: e.g., "CBC missing" Reason: "Entire test category performed" 3. TESTCAT empty, individual TESTDE missing (e.g., WBC count missing) Condition: TESTCAT empty, TESTDAT empty, ORRES empty Output: One record missing TESTDE per subject/visit/TESTCAT Format: e.g., "CBC-WBC Count missing" Reason: "Test result empty" Note: Input data must prepared prepare_test_data, standardizes column names SUBJID, VISIT, VISITNUM, SVDAT, TESTCAT, TESTDE, TESTYN, TESTDAT, TESTTIM, ORRES, etc.

title: Check Missing Tests ŌĆö check_missing_test
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Step 1: Read config and prepare test data testconfig <- read_testconfig_file("test_config.xlsx") prepared_lb <- prepare_test_data(   data = data,   test_dataset = "LB",   test_date_var = "LBDAT",   test_result_var = "ORRES" )  # Step 2: Check all missing tests result <- check_missing_test(data = prepared_lb) print(result)  # Check specific test only result <- check_missing_test(   data = prepared_lb,   test_var = "TESTDE",   test = "RBC Count" )  # Only check overall missing, skip individual indicators result <- check_missing_test(data = prepared_lb, missing_de = FALSE) } # }

NULL, function look variable named visitcode calling environment. found, error raised. Must contain columns: VISIT, VISITNUM (numeric), WP, CYCLE, VISITDAY, type, wpvalue (numeric) ex_datasets Character vector, exposure dataset names (default: "EX"). Multiple datasets can specified, e.g., c("EX1", "EX2") ex_date_var Character vector, dosing start date variable names (default: "EXSTDAT"). length 1, datasets use column name length equals ex_datasets, corresponds one--one datasets Example: c("EXSTDAT1", "EXSTDAT2") corresponds c("EX1", "EX2") ex_end_date_var Character vector, dosing end date variable names (default: NULL). NULL empty, last dose date uses ex_date_var (dosing start date) specified, last dose date uses dosing end date length 1, datasets use column name length equals ex_datasets, corresponds one--one datasets sv_dataset Character string, visit dataset name (default: "SV") sv_visit_var Character string, visit name variable visit dataset (default: "VISIT") sv_visitnum_var Character string, visit number variable visit dataset (default: "VISITNUM") sv_date_var Character string, visit date variable visit dataset (default: "SVDAT") eot_dataset Character vector, end treatment dataset names (default: "EOT"). Multiple datasets can specified; latest EOT date per subject used. dataset exists, EOT-related planned dates NA eot_date_var Character vector, end treatment date variable names (default: "EOTDAT"). length 1, datasets use column name length equals eot_dataset, corresponds one--one datasets ds_dataset Character string, disposition dataset name (default: "DS"). dataset exist, EOS-related planned dates NA ds_date_var Character string, end study date variable (default: "DSDAT") tb_name_var Character string specifying variable name use TBNAME output (default: NULL). title: Generate Planned Visit Dates ŌĆö generate_planned_visit_dates
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Prepare visit schedule data visit_schedule <- data.frame(   VISIT = c("Screening", "C1D1", "C1D8", "C2D1", "EOT"),   VISITNUM = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 99),   WP = c("", "+/-3d", "+/-2d", "+/-3d", "+7d"),   CYCLE = c("Screening", "Cycle 1", "Cycle 1", "Cycle 2", "End of Treatment"),   VISITDAY = c("0", "1", "8", "1", "EOT"),   type = c(NA, "+/-", "+/-", "+/-", "+"),   wpvalue = c(NA, 3, 2, 3, 7),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Prepare clinical trial data data <- list(   EX = data.frame(     SUBJID = c("001", "001", "002"),     EXSTDAT = c("2024-01-01", "2024-01-29", "2024-01-05"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   SV = data.frame(     SUBJID = c("001", "001", "002"),     VISIT = c("C1D1", "C1D8", "C1D1"),     VISITNUM = c(1, 2, 1),     SVDAT = c("2024-01-01", "2024-01-08", "2024-01-05"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   EOT = data.frame(     SUBJID = c("001", "002"),     EOTDAT = c("2024-03-15", "2024-03-20"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   DS = data.frame(     SUBJID = c("001", "002"),     DSDAT = c("2024-04-15", "2024-04-20"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) )  # Generate planned visit dates (using default visitcode variable) visitcode <- visit_schedule result <- generate_planned_visit_dates(data = data)  # Or explicitly pass visitcode result <- generate_planned_visit_dates(   data = data,   visitcode = visit_schedule )  # Uniform 21-day cycle (overrides CYCDAY in visitcode) result <- generate_planned_visit_dates(   data = data,   visitcode = visit_schedule,   cycle_days = 21 )  # Per-cycle intervals from visitcode CYCDAY column (cycle_days = NULL) result <- generate_planned_visit_dates(data = data, visitcode = visitcode)  # With multiple exposure datasets result <- generate_planned_visit_dates(   data = data,   visitcode = visit_schedule,   ex_datasets = c("EX1", "EX2"),   ex_date_var = c("EXSTDAT1", "EXSTDAT2") ) } # }

title: Prepare Test Data for Missing Test Checks ŌĆö prepare_test_data
text: data List containing clinical trial datasets test_dataset Character string, test dataset name extract (e.g., "LB") test_date_var Character string, original test date variable (default: "LBDAT") test_time_var Character string, original test time variable (default: NULL). NULL empty, TESTTIM column NA test_yn_var Character string, original test performed variable (default: "YN") test_result_var Character string, original test result variable (default: "ORRES") test_cat_var Character string, original test category variable (default: "LBCAT"). NULL empty, TESTCAT column NA test_de_var Character string, original test name variable (default: NULL). NULL empty, TESTDE column NA. Used specific test names tb_name_var Character string, original dataset name variable (default: NULL). NULL empty, TBNAME column uses test_dataset value (e.g., "LB") sv_dataset Character string, visit dataset name (default: "SV") sv_visit_var Character string, visit name variable visit dataset (default: "VISIT") sv_visitnum_var Character string, visit number variable visit dataset (default: "VISITNUM") sv_date_var Character string, visit date variable visit dataset (default: "SVDAT") config Data frame, test-visit configuration (required, default: NULL). NULL, function look variable named testconfig calling environment; found, error raised. Must data frame, typically created read_testconfig_file expands VISITNUM one per row. Config must contain: TESTCAT (test category), VISITNUM (visit number, one per row). Generates expected visit-test combinations skeleton, showing empty records (TESTDAT=NA) even original data records. Use read_testconfig_file read config Excel/CSV files config_cat Character vector, test categories filter config (default: NULL). NULL, uses TESTCAT config; provided (e.g., c("CBC", "Chemistry")), uses TESTCAT records filter_cond Character string, subject filter condition (default: NULL). multiple conditions separated semicolons (intersection/logic): "ENROL|ENRYN=='Y'" - Filter enrolled subjects "SUBJECT|SEX=='M'" - Filter males SUBJECT dataset "SUBJECT|AGE>=18" - Filter age >= 18 SUBJECT "ENROL|ENRYN=='Y';SUBJECT|SEX=='M'" - Enrolled males (intersection) NULL, subject filtering applied
